annotations.label: Bayesian probability

anchor.snippet: is an interpretation of the concept of probability

annotations.note: The philosophical interpretation of probability as degree of belief is not a formal mathematical object and has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Prior and posterior probability

anchor.snippet: the Bayesian probabilist specifies a prior probability

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.posterior κ μ` is the posterior Markov kernel obtained from a prior measure `μ` and likelihood kernel `κ`, the measure-theoretic form of the prior/posterior concept.

annotations.label: Sequential use of Bayes' theorem

anchor.snippet: calculate the posterior distribution using Bayes' theorem

annotations.note: Bayes' theorem (`cond_eq_inv_mul_cond_mul`) and iterated conditioning (`cond_cond_eq_cond_inter`, conditioning on `s` then `t` equals conditioning on `s∩t`) are formalized, but the sequential-updating methodology to obtain posterior distributions is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Objective Bayesian probability

anchor.snippet: who interpret probability as an extension of logic

annotations.note: The objective/logical interpretation of probability is an informal philosophical stance with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subjective Bayesian probability

anchor.snippet: probability corresponds to a personal belief

annotations.note: The subjective (personal-belief) interpretation of probability is informal philosophy and is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bayes' original special case

anchor.snippet: the prior and posterior distributions were beta distributions

annotations.note: The beta distribution is defined (`betaPDF`/`betaMeasure`), but the Beta–Binomial conjugacy showing prior and posterior are both beta distributions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cox's axioms for Bayesian updating

anchor.snippet: Bayesian updating follows from several axioms

annotations.note: Cox's theorem deriving probability/Bayesian updating from plausibility axioms has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dutch book

anchor.snippet: makes a Dutch book by setting the odds and bets

annotations.note: The Dutch book / coherence betting argument is not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wald's complete class theorem

anchor.snippet: every admissible statistical procedure is either a Bayesian procedure or a limit of Bayesian procedures

annotations.note: A statistical-decision-theory framework with Bayes risk and minimax risk exists, but admissibility is not defined and Wald's complete class theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bayesian procedures are admissible
annotations.note: Bayes risk is defined in the decision-theory framework, but admissibility of decision procedures is not defined and the admissibility-of-Bayes-procedures result is not formalized.
